title: Terminal session manager with tmux integration and storage reconciliation
purpose: Manages terminal sessions using tmux, handles session storage persistence, and reconciles stored sessions with actual tmux sessions.
module_context: Exports a singleton TerminalManager instance that provides methods for reading/writing terminal session state, generating tmux session names, creating and killing tmux sessions, listing active sessions, and reconciling session states.

steps.content: Check for the existence of a new storage path in the .collab/sessions/ directory.
IF the new path exists or its directory exists, return the new path.
ELSE check for the existence of an old storage path in the .collab/ directory for backwards compatibility.
IF the old path exists or its directory exists, return the old path.
ELSE return the new path as the default location.
Determine the storage path for the terminal sessions.
IF the storage file does not exist, return an empty session state with current timestamp.
ELSE read and parse the content of the storage file as JSON.
IF parsing fails, log a warning and return an empty session state with current timestamp.
RETURN the parsed session state.

methods.name: writeSessions
methods.normalized_params: (project: string, session: string, state: TerminalSessionsState): Promise<void>

steps.content: Determine the storage path for the terminal sessions.
Create the directory for the storage path if it does not exist.
Update the last modified timestamp in the session state.
Write the session state to the storage path as a formatted JSON string.
Extract the session name from the collab session identifier by splitting on ':' or '/' and taking the last segment.
IF the extracted segment is empty, default to 'default'.
Sanitize the session name by converting to lowercase and removing all non-alphanumeric characters.
Truncate the sanitized name to a maximum of 20 characters.
Generate a random 4-character suffix using base-36 encoding.
Combine the prefix 'mc-', the sanitized name, and the random suffix to form the tmux session name.
Create a new tmux session in detached mode with the specified session name and optional working directory.
Enable mouse scrolling for the newly created tmux session.
Attempt to unbind the horizontal split key for the tmux session.
IF unbinding the horizontal split key fails, continue without throwing an error.
Attempt to unbind the vertical split key for the tmux session.
IF unbinding the vertical split key fails, continue without throwing an error.
IF the session creation fails due to a duplicate session, enable mouse mode and attempt to unbind split keys for the existing session.
IF the session creation fails due to a duplicate session, return without throwing an error.
IF the session creation fails for any other reason, throw an error with the failure details.
Attempt to kill the specified tmux session.
IF killing the session fails due to a 'no session' error, return without throwing an error.
IF killing the session fails for any other reason, throw an error with the failure details.
Execute a command to list all active tmux sessions with their names.
IF the command fails due to no tmux server, return an empty array.
Parse the command output to extract session names.
Filter the session names to include only those that start with the specified prefix.
RETURN the filtered list of session names.
Read the stored terminal sessions from the file.
List all active tmux sessions that match the prefix 'mc-{session}-'.
Identify stored sessions that do not have corresponding active tmux sessions.
Identify active tmux sessions that do not have corresponding stored sessions.
Kill any active tmux sessions that are not in the stored sessions.
Remove orphaned stored sessions from the session state.
Write the updated session state back to the file.
Log a summary of the reconciliation process including the number of orphan records removed and orphan tmux sessions killed.
